title: salvia microphylla var. neurepia
intro: Salvia microphylla var. neurepia, also known as Purple Sage, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico and South America, belonging to the mint family and recognized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and purple flowers. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of Purple Sage include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, relieve stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system, while also exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Purple Sage is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be added to salads and other culinary dishes for its flavor and nutritional value.
